title: Navigation of autonomous vehicles to enhance safety under one or more fault conditions
abstract: Various embodiments relate generally to autonomous vehicles and associated mechanical, electrical and electronic hardware, computing software, including autonomy applications, image processing applications, etc., and computing systems, and wired and wireless network communications to facilitate autonomous control of vehicles, and, more specifically, to systems, devices, and methods configured to navigate autonomous vehicles under one or more fault conditions. In some examples, a method may include localizing an autonomous vehicle, accessing map data to identify safe zones, computing drive parameters and alternate drive parameters, detecting an anomalous event, and apply alternate drive parameters to a vehicle control unit.
claims:
Claims In the claims: 1. A method comprising: localizing an autonomous vehicle relative to a roadway over which the autonomous vehicle is configured to transit via a path of travel; accessing map data to identify locations of one or more safe zones associated with the path of travel; computing drive parameters with which to apply to a vehicle controller to facilitate transit of the autonomous vehicle coextensive with the path of travel; computing one or more subsets of alternate drive parameters with which to apply to the vehicle controller to facilitate transit of the autonomous vehicle to a safe zone via a recovery path of travel; detecting an anomalous event; and applying a subset of the alternate drive parameters to guide the autonomous vehicle to the safe zone. 2. The method of claim 1 further comprising: receiving images of the locations of the one or more safe zones captured via one or more cameras; and analyzing the images and the map data to confirm the locations of the one or more safe zones. 3. The method of claim 2 further comprising: analyzing the images and the map data at multiple points along the path of travel; identifying another safe zone based on the map data; determining data representing an occlusion associated with the another safe zone; and excluding the another safe zone from being selected as the safe zone prior to detecting the anomalous event. 4. The method of claim 2 wherein analyzing the images and the map data comprises: receiving images of the locations as high definition ("HD") images; and analyzing the high definition mages and high definition ("HD") map data, which constitutes as the map data. 5. The method of claim 1 further comprising: selecting a location for one of the one or more safe zones prior to detecting the anomalous event. 6. The method of claim 5 wherein selecting the location comprises: identifying a reference point representing a location of the autonomous vehicle; and predicting a subset of actions to guide the autonomous vehicle autonomously to the safe zone, at least one action including the subset of the alternate drive parameters. 7. The method of claim 6 wherein predicting the subset of actions comprises: determining for the at least one action the subset of the alternate drive parameters to navigate the autonomous vehicle over a portion of the recovery path of travel that excludes an object on the roadway. 8. The method of claim 1 further comprising: classifying the detected anomalous event based on a state of operation of a sensor; identifying one or more other sensors responsive to the state of operation of the sensor; and determining an event-specific subset of actions implementing the one or more other sensors based on the detected anomalous event. 9. The method of claim 8 further comprising: implementing the event-specific subset of actions to navigate the autonomous vehicle to the safe zone via the recovery path. 10. The method of claim 1 further comprising: identifying data representing a glide path associated with the safe zone, the data representing the glide path including waypoints each of which is associated with executable instructions configured to receive sensor data and to guide the autonomous vehicle from a waypoint to the safe zone. 11. The method of claim 11 further comprising: receiving a portion of the map data as updated map data including the executable instructions of at least one waypoint of the glide path; and performing the executable instructions to navigate via the glide path. 12. The method of claim 1 further comprising: receiving images of the locations of the one or more safe zones captured via one or more cameras; analyzing the images and the map data at multiple points along the path of travel; identifying another safe zone based on the map data; determining data representing an occlusion associated with the another safe zone; and 

 excluding the another safe zone from being selected as the safe zone prior to detecting the anomalous event. 13. The method of claim 12 further comprising: transmitting the data representing the occlusion via a network to a computing device implemented as a portion of a vehicular autonomy platform to cause an update to a standard map. 14. The method of claim 1 further comprising: detecting activation of a vehicular drive control after detecting the anomalous event; and countermanding application of the subset of the alternate drive parameters. 15. The method of claim 14 further comprising: identifying a state in which a driver is intervening with the transit via the recovery path of travel; and invalidating control signals to facilitate application of the subset of the alternate drive parameters. 16. The method of claim 1 further comprising: receiving images of regions associated with the path of travel captured via one or more cameras; analyzing the images of at least one of the regions; detecting one or more objects at the at least one region to form an obstructed region; and identifying the safe zone as another region in which objects are absent. 17. An apparatus comprising: a memory including executable instructions; and a processor, responsive to executing the instructions, is configured to: localize an autonomous vehicle relative to a roadway to transit via a path of travel; access map data to identify locations of one or more safe zones associated with the path of travel; compute drive parameters with which to apply to a vehicle controller to facilitate transit via the path of travel; compute one or more subsets of alternate drive parameters to facilitate transit to a safe zone via a recovery path of travel; detect an anomalous event; and 

 apply a subset of the alternate drive parameters to guide the autonomous vehicle to the safe zone. 18. The apparatus of claim 17, wherein the processor is further configured to: receive images of the locations of the one or more safe zones captured via one or more cameras; and analyze the images and the map data to confirm the locations of the one or more safe zones. 19. The apparatus of claim 18, wherein the processor is further configured to: analyze the images and the map data at multiple points along the path of travel; identify another safe zone based on the map data; determine data representing an occlusion associated with the another safe zone; and exclude the another safe zone from being selected as the safe zone prior to detecting the anomalous event. 20. The apparatus of claim 17, wherein the processor is further configured to: classify the detected anomalous event based on a state of operation of a sensor; identify one or more other sensors responsive to the state of operation of the sensor; determine an event-specific subset of actions implementing the one or more other sensors based on the detected anomalous event; and implement the event-specific subset of actions to navigate the autonomous vehicle to the safe zone via the recovery path.
